summary: Comprehensive testing of WebGL visual engine implementation using React Three Fiber and Three.js. All core requirements verified successfully across login, onboarding, and dashboard pages.
passed_tests: Visual engine div with data-testid='visual-engine' exists in DOM
Canvas element inside visual-engine div exists and has full viewport dimensions (1920x1080)
Visual engine div has position:fixed, inset:0, z-index:0, pointer-events:none
UI layer has position:relative and z-index:10 (higher than visual engine)
Visual engine renders correctly on /login page with atmospheric dark environment
Visual engine renders correctly on /onboarding page with welcome content overlay
Visual engine renders correctly on /dashboard page with complex UI layout
Dashboard layout completely intact: top-bar, left-column attention/projects, ai-core-placeholder, right-column schedule/tasks
All dashboard content present: 3 attention items, 3 active projects, 4 trades, 4 schedule items, 4 urgent tasks, 4 financial stats
All interactive elements remain clickable: login form inputs, buttons, dashboard buttons not blocked by canvas
Login form fully functional: email input, password input, sign in button, Google button all interactive
Full navigation flow works: login → onboarding → dashboard → back to login with visual engine persisting
No JavaScript errors blocking the page functionality
WebGL canvas properly transparent with alpha:true allowing UI overlay
GPU performance warnings normal for WebGL context (expected behavior)
Visual engine styling consistent across all pages
UI content loads successfully on all tested pages
